title: System-Wide Stats

story.i_want_to: view system-wide aggregate statistics across all tenants in a single API call
story.so_that: I can monitor platform health, understand usage patterns, and identify capacity needs without querying each tenant individually

rationale: Platform operators need a bird's-eye view of system usage for capacity planning, monitoring, and business metrics. Querying each tenant individually is impractical at scale. A single aggregate stats endpoint provides immediate visibility into total tenants, projects, stories, agents, and story status distribution across the entire platform.

acceptance_criteria.description: GET /api/v1/admin/stats returns a JSON object with system-wide aggregate statistics. Requires superadmin API key.
The response includes total counts: total_tenants, total_projects, total_stories, total_epics, total_agents, total_api_keys.
The response includes tenant status breakdown: tenants_active, tenants_suspended, tenants_deactivated.
The response includes story status aggregates: stories_by_agent_status (pending, assigned, implementing, reported_done counts) and stories_by_verified_status (unverified, verified, rejected counts).
The response includes active_stories count (stories with agent_status='implementing').
The response includes active_agents count (agents with status='active' and last_seen_at within the last 24 hours).
The stats query bypasses RLS to aggregate across all tenants. This is inherent to superadmin's NULL tenant_id context.

test_cases.name: active_agents counts only recently seen agents
expected_results: active_agents is 1 (only the agent seen within last 24 hours)

test_cases.name: stats with empty system
preconditions: No tenants, projects, stories, or agents exist

technical_notes: Create AdminStatsController with show action. Route: GET /api/v1/admin/stats.
Use a single optimized query or a few targeted queries to gather all stats. Avoid N+1 by not iterating tenants.
Example SQL pattern: SELECT count(*) as total, count(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'active') as active FROM tenants.

For cross-tenant queries, the superadmin's NULL tenant_id means RLS policies with tenant_id checks will not restrict the query. Ensure RLS policies allow NULL tenant_id superadmin to SELECT across all rows, or bypass RLS for superadmin connections.
Story status aggregates can use GROUP BY with count: SELECT agent_status, count(*) FROM stories GROUP BY agent_status.
active_agents query: WHERE status = 'active' AND last_seen_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours'.
Response is a flat JSON object (not paginated) since it's a single aggregate record.
Consider caching stats for a short TTL (e.g., 60 seconds) if the queries become expensive at scale.
All test modules MUST use `async: true` (e.g., `use Loopctl.DataCase, async: true` or `use LoopctlWeb.ConnCase, async: true`)
All tests using Mox MUST include `setup :verify_on_exit!`
All mocks MUST be defined centrally in `test/support/mocks.ex`, never in individual test files
All context functions MUST return `{:ok, result}` or `{:error, reason}` tuples. Never raise exceptions for expected error cases.
